text_source: ....Whew. That about does it for all my errands.
It's already past noon. No point in overworking\n
myself... Guess I'll take a little break.
I walk out into the hallway, patting my empty\n
stomach.
Guess I'll get something for lunch--Whoa!?
Just as I take a step forward, I step on\n
something round, and I slip.
Geez, that was close. Almost broke my head open\n
there. What is this...? A potato?
Worker
Is that...?
I look around to see one of the workers\n
walking away, holding a basket full of these\n
tubers.
Did she accidentally drop it...?
As I stand there, thinking, the worker makes\n
her way to the back door and goes outside.
...And there she goes.
Guess I can just give it to her later.
What exactly is this, though...? I don't think\n
I've seen any crop like this around here.
I wonder how you're supposed to eat it.
I turn the potatoey thing over and over in my\n
hands as I continue to think.
Oh, I know--Maybe Kuon knows what this might\n
be.
She is an apothecary, after all. Edible plants\n
should be right up her alley... I'll head\n
over now.
...I place my hand on the door to Kuon's room.
Kuon, you got a moment?
But Kuon is staring straight at her mortar,\n
fully focused on the task at hand.
...Making some medicine there?
Kuon?
Her face intent and focused, she continues mixing\n
the contents of the mortar.
Ah, well. Guess I'll wait until she's finished.
...Whew.
All done?
Mhm, that should be good for now.
Sorry about that. I just couldn't let my\n
attention wander for a moment.
Don't worry about it. What were you so absorbed\n
in making, anyway?
This? It's for my flash bombs. I'm running low,\n
so I thought I'd make more. You'd recognize it,\n
I think?
Oh, huh... I remember now. Didn't know you\n
made these by hand. You saved my life with\n
these back then...
Mhm. It's made with a secret recipe of my\n
very own.
A secret recipe, eh?
Mhm. Just as it sounds, it's a secret,\n
so I can't really explain in detail.
Kuon closes up the canister and puts it at her\n
side. I peek in... She has several more bombs\n
now.
You sure made a lot of them. Do you really\n
need this many?
Well, I didn't have much of the ingredients\n
left. I decided I might as well use the last\n
of it.
At any rate, was there something you needed\n
from me, Haku?
Well, I finished all my work, so I thought\n
I'd come by to chat for a little break.
Oh, by the way, you have any clue what this is?
I take out the potato-like vegetable that the\n
worker dropped earlier.
Oh... A mororo. Now that's some unexpected\n
nostalgia... Where did you find it?
I picked it up along the way. A mororo, huh?
Mhm. It's a staple food in my homeland.\n
I didn't think anyone around here ate these...
Kuon smiles, her expression distant and soft,\n
as though she's remembering her homeland.
Wonder why she had this if it's so rare, then.
Oh, Haku, why don't you stay for a cup of... hm?
Looks like I'm out at the moment. Hold on,\n
I'll go get some more tea.
She doesn't really have to go to all that\n
trouble...
Hmm hmm, hmhmmmm...
Huh?
I look outside the window, hearing humming\n
coming from the garden.
There, I see the worker from before piling\n
fallen leaves in the center. She seems to be in\n
high spirits.
She seems pretty happy about something...
I look closer to see several mororo sticking\n
out of the leaves.
Mhm. This should do just fine. All that's left\n
is to kindle the fire...
Hm? A little damp...? Ah, such carelessness is\n
inexcusable... I should have aired them out.
Looks like she's trying to start a fire there.\n
Is she going to heat the mororos with it?
A sudden idea comes to mind, and my gaze turns\n
toward the middle of the room.
She wants a... fire, huh? If she's having\n
trouble lighting it, I guess I could help.
Kuon's secret mixture, and the fire in the\n
garden... Out of boredom, I come up with a plan.
...This'll be perfect to kill some time.\n
I'll just borrow some of this, Kuon...
I take a pinch of the mixture from the mortar\n
and mix it with the clay that was used for its\n
base.
This much should be enough to just light the\n
fire... I think? Could be dangerous if I use\n
too much.
I'll just roll it up like this...\n
Perfect.
Satisfied, I look again to the window that\n
faces the garden.
Gotta aim for the center of the leaves...\n
There!
Just as I throw it down, she seems to finish\n
her preparations to light the pile.
That should do. Now all that remains is to\n
blow some air on it...
She takes a deep breath.
Fwooooooooo!!
As the air hits it, flames begin to ripple\n
across the leaves. And that's when the clay\n
hits--
Hm? It looked as though something strange\n
fell in...
*CRACK*
Wha--!?
A blinding light fills the garden, far beyond\n
what I had expected.
Holy crap, that stuff's powerful... I didn't\n
expect such a small amount to be this potent.
Wait, is she OK!?
She didn't fall into the fire, did she...?\n
I quickly look back at the garden.
...S-Such carelessness is... inexcusable...\n
I had no idea mororos these days emit such\n
light...
...Oh, but I must put out the fire!\n
I cannot allow it to spread further!
Yet I cannot see right now... In that case...!
She holds the bamboo pipe--the one she was\n
blowing air through--like a sword, and\n
lowers her stance, listening.
A sudden tension fills the air in the garden...
Whoa, this air she gives off...\n
She's no ordinary servant...!
...Found it. There!
With a swift slash, the bamboo pipe blows apart\n
the fire and puts it out... but...
Uh...
*Whoooooosh!*
The bamboo pipe knocks one of the mororo into\n
a miraculous arc straight up into the air.
Hm. That should have extingui--\n
*Thunk*
Oh...
And somehow, the airborne mororo comes crashing\n
inexorably down on the worker's head, as if\n
drawn to her.
...S-Such carelessness... inexcusable...\n
What a blunder...
The garden is empty, except for the worker\n
collapsed on the ground, and what remains\n
of the fire.
...I'll just... pretend I never saw any of that.
Together with Kuon and the others, our patrol\n
takes us across the main street to the vendors.
Peaceful as always...
The hustle and bustle of the crowds is the same\n
as usual, but there's no arguing or grumbling.
Oh... It's Atuy.
Hm?
I follow Nekone's gaze. It's hard to see through\n
all the people, but I notice Atuy in high\n
spirits.
...Didn't she say she was going to stay in her\n
room because she had a stomach ache?
What is she doing...? It looks like she's\n
talking to that guy sitting in the street.
Could that man... be ill, perhaps?
I... wonder if something's happened.
Are you all right? Here, have some water.
Sick man
Thank you so much... My body simply fails me\n
sometimes... and I have these sudden attacks...
The man brushes his hair up as he accepts the\n
water from Atuy, apparently to take his\n
medicine.
That sounds awfully tough...\n
Is your home close by?
Yes, it is only a short walk from here.
I'll walk you back, then.
No, I--I would dare not impose so on someone\n
I have only just met, milady fair...
The man tries to stand, but he staggers as his\n
leg gives way, forced to lean against Atuy.
Oh...
H-Here. Come on, pet, you can't even stand.\n
It'd be dangerous for you to walk home all\n
by yourself.
My apologies... I do not wish to be a burden,\n
but may I rely on you to see me safely home...?
Of course I don't mind.
Atuy lends the man a hand, propping him up gently.
Whew... Thank you... Kind miss, may I have the\n
honor of knowing your name?
My name's Atuy.
Atuy... Ah, such a wonderful name. There is\n
something so resonant to it... like a vast\n
flowing ocean.
So kind... beautiful, and accepting of all\n
things, like the mother ocean...\n
A truly fitting name.
The man smiles softly, gazing at Atuy.
I-I don't think... anybody's ever said\n
something like that... to me...
What? That seems... unthinkable.
That even in the renowned imperial capital,\n
there are still lowborn who do not recognize\n
true beauty...
Ooh, my... That's far too much praise,\n
really...
Atuy seems rather pleased with this, blushing\n
madly.
Ah... Perhaps it was fate that I had fallen\n
ill here... for it allowed me to meet you.
Yes, it must be... It was destiny that the two\n
of us were able to find each other here.
Seems like an anemic noble boy or something.\n
How can he say such embarrassing crap like\n
that with a straight face?
Man, this is awful. What kind of woman would\n
ever fall for these ridiculous pick-up lines...?
...Wait a minute. Why is Atuy blushing like that?
...Don't tell me she goes for pretentious guys\n
like him?
Uh... Erm, well, to each their own, as they say.\n
We don't have the right to judge others.
...That is correct.
Eh, guess you're right.
Well, I'm getting hungry. Let's get moving.\n
Once patrol's done, we should get some dinner.
I begin walking away, but the other three remain\n
still, continuing to watch Atuy.
What's the matter?
Well, as a friend, I feel it's my responsibility\n
to see this through to the end.
Yes. It is our duty.
Um, I... I think so... as well...
Are you guys serious...?
Is your house this way?
Yes. It is not far from here.
Atuy keeps supporting the man as the two of them\n
make their way down the road.
...And a ways behind them, the girls shuffle\n
along, paragons of stealth.
You know...
Shh... They'll notice us.
Why are we even doing this...?
Kuon and the others seem awfully interested\n
in this, but their faces are all pretty serious.
It doesn't look like they're doing this just\n
for kicks. Guess they're all worried about Atuy.
That said...
After getting to know Atuy, there's some things\n
I've come to realize.
She may seem carefree and air-headed, but like\n
Kuon, she's, uh... Nope. Not going to think\n
about it.
...Suffice it to say, I highly doubt a skinny\n
guy like that could do anything to Atuy...
Oh... beautiful... flatter me...
Of... not... your... know no bounds...
We catch bits and pieces of their conversation\n
at our distance. The man's spouting more\n
sweet nothings.
Is he really that sick? He sure talks a lot\n
for how he was acting before. It seems weird...\n
Huh?
Wait a minute. Is that why Kuon's...?
As I think, Atuy turns to the right, and stops\n
in front of a vast mansion.
This is my manor. Thank you so much for\n
walking me here, Atuy.
It's only natural to help out if you see\n
someone in trouble!
But not many take such ideology beyond empty\n
words. Truly, you must have a kind heart indeed.
Attendant-like man
Young Master, welcome home.
Yes, I have just now returned.
Oh? And who might this be?
She helped me when I suffered an attack in the\n
market. I owe her my life. Please treat her\n
with the utmost respect.
Miss Atuy, if you don't mind, would you care\n
to stop in? I dearly wish to convey my full\n
thanks.
Oh, no, I really ought to be going now.\n
Please don't mind me.
I see. Well then, Atuy?
The man takes Atuy's hand in his.
Wh, ah, yes!?
Would you meet with me again soon?\n
I believe destiny brought us to each other.\n
Fate cannot be denied.
Ah...
Or am I... being a bother to you?
N-No, of course not!
Then...
Mhm...
Well, if you insist so much, I'll be happy\n
to oblige.
Ah, what joy you have given humble Chalafun!\n
I have not felt such happiness in years.\n
May I ask where I can find you?
I'm staying over at a place called the Hakurokaku\n
on the outskirts of the capital.
Chalafun
Ah, I know of it. If it allows me to see you\n
again, I will happily visit. Until then...
Oh, uh, yes. I'll... be waiting.
Atuy...
...Something is not right here.
If memory serves me correctly, this manor is\n
currently listed as vacant.
Nekone looks at the other two with a puzzled\n
expression.
...I'd like you to elaborate a bit, I think.
The other day, my dear brother received orders\n
to conduct a census survey of this area.
At that time, this manor was completely empty.
Um, Miss Atuy is heading this way...
We quickly hide in the shadows as Atuy walks\n
towards our direction.
Nekone, what are the chances that someone moved in\n
between that survey and now?
It was mere days ago, so I believe it incredibly\n
unlikely.
Which means those people over there are most\n
likely up to no good, I'd assume.
Hold it, Kuon. Where do you think you're going?
I was considering beating the truth out of them\n
right now, actually.
Or are you going to try to stop me?
Holy crap, what!? Yeesh, she's terrifying when\n
she gets like this. I know how you feel, but...
You need to calm down, Kuon. It's not like you\n
to charge in blindly.
We still don't know who these guys are.\n
For stuff like this, we need more information\n
first.
It's hard to remember because of how she acts,\n
but Atuy is the daughter of a fancy noble family.
I don't know if we should just crash in before\n
finding out WHY these guys are after her.
Anyway, I just think we should find out more first.\n
Investigate the mansion, tail them, all that.
...You might be right, I think.
Whew, looks like she's calmed down a bit...
Let's split up for now. Nekone, you know tons\n
about the capital--you and Kuon scout the area.
Understood.
Rulutieh and I will watch the manor from here.
As the sun sets, and lights glow within houses\n
across the city, the young man and the valet\n
emerge.
Kuon and Nekone aren't back yet... Guess there's\n
nothing for it. We'd better follow them.
Rulutieh and I tail the two until they go into\n
a pub on the city's outskirts.
This pub seems a little shady for some rich boy\n
to be visiting...
I'll pretend to be a customer and head in.\n
Rulutieh, I want you to head back to the\n
Hakurokaku.
Um, may I... not accompany you... inside,\n
Sir Haku?
I know she wants to help Atuy, but she'll stick\n
out like a sore thumb in a hole-in-the-wall place\n
like this...
Well, if I go in a place like this with a girl,\n
we might get unwanted attention. It sucks, but...
Oh... I'm sorry. I suppose that was a little\n
selfish of me... Please, be careful...
Rulutieh smiles sadly, but she quickly hides it,\n
murmuring only a word of caution for me.
It'll be a walk in the park. All I have to do is\n
buy a drink and listen in. Nothing to worry about.
Yes, but still... Please, be careful...
The "young master" seems to have joined a bunch of\n
ruffians. They sit in the back, ordering drinks.
Man
So, how'd it go, boss?
Heh, easy. She melted like butter. Fell for it\n
immediately, she did.
The slim man laughs crudely, his tone confident.\n
He explains what happened to his apparent\n
companions.
And so, off comes his pretty little mask.\n
Just like that...
...So I even got her to promise to meet up with\n
me again.
Attendant
So wha'bout this girl? Think we'll be able to\n
squeeze any cash outta her?
And of course, that one's no valet.
Yeah. She's dressed fancy, and if she's staying\n
at the Hakurokaku, she must be loaded, right?
Must be from some rich family from the boonies.\n
Who else talks in that hick Miyako dialect\n
these days?
Heheheh, seriously. Well, that just means she's\n
gonna be all the easier to rope in.
So what happens next?
Our strategy all comes down to whatever they're\n
planning. Go on, let's hear it all.
Biggest catch we've ever had. With all that cash,\n
I'll marry in and live like a king for years.
Means we'll be able to lay low for a while,\n
then.
True. We've been conning people left and right\n
lately. The guards are starting to get antsy.
All right. Next time, you guys ambush her.\n
I'll jump in, save her, and she'll fall all\n
over me.
I mean, this sickly little noble's riskin' his\n
life for her. What lady could resist?
Man, you've never been sick a day in your life,\n
and you still look like a frail little rich boy.
That's the only reason this shit keeps working\n
so well. Thank your lucky stars I'm such a\n
pretty-boy.
Yeah, sure. Just don't forget we've been\n
handling the dirty work.
'Course I won't. Once I'm married and everything,\n
you lads'll get your share.
Glad to hear it. That cash we got from selling\n
off the last girl is starting to run out.
So they've gotten to others before...
I take a swig of the drink--predictably, it's\n
awful--and then leave my money on the table.
Heh, these girls fall for this shit every time.
Gahahahaha! You got that right!
Glad Kuon wasn't here this time.
Just remembering her expression earlier is\n
already giving me the heebie-jeebies.
You should all be thankful she's not here.
I leave behind the pub and the men's crude\n
laughter as I make my way out.
Doesn't seem like they were specifically\n
targeting Atuy...
Well that makes my job a little easier.\n
Now, how to explain all this to Kuon and\n
the others?
Makes me feel like I'm siccing a crazed tiger\n
on someone...
Still, they're the ones who trod on its tail.\n
Everyone gets what's coming to them.
...Problem is, I need to figure out how to\n
explain this to Atuy.
Hm... Well... Guess I'll just let things play out.
It'll all work out... probably.
Around sunset, a message from the slim man\n
arrives. Atuy begins dressing up, beaming.
...Well, someone's in a good mood.
Hee hee, well, I happened to help out a stranger,\n
and they said they'd treat me to dinner.
Oh yeah? Sounds like a real gentleman.
Oh, he IS. He was such a dreamboat!\n
This could be the start of my very own\n
love story.
This love of hers is going to be over before\n
it even starts... but I can't say that to her\n
face.
Really now? Well, hope you have a nice night\n
out.
Thanks. I'm going to try my best.
I see the giddy Atuy off to the front door\n
and watch her leave.
...*Sigh* I still feel guilty about this\n
whole thing.
Shall we get going as well, then?
Whoa--Geez, would you not sneak up on me like\n
that?
Remember the plan. Hide, follow Atuy, and if\n
anything bad happens, we act. That would be\n
best, I think.
I'm sure Atuy can handle anything that comes\n
her way, but we should be prepared for the\n
worst.
Kuon's smiling as she speaks, but her tail\n
flicks and twists in short, violent motions\n
as well.
That's usually a sign she's in a bad mood.
She seems really worked up about all this...
She's been like this ever since I explained what\n
I heard about those guys' plans.
You know, if Haku had apprehended those men\n
on the spot, we would not have to go through\n
all this.
It's one thing to confront them AFTER they\n
actually do something, but doing it BEFORE\n
gets tricky.
And you're asking the impossible! How can I\n
"apprehend" them alone? They'd beat the shit\n
out of me.
Um, dear sister...
Are you sure we should not inform Atuy about\n
this...? If her feelings are hurt...
If we don't have any proof to back up what we're\n
saying, she might not even believe us.
...Hm. You do have a point. I do not believe that\n
Atuy has full trust in you yet, Haku.
People tend not to believe in truths that might\n
mean bad things for them.
And what if she asks HIM and he says I misheard\n
them? Then this would just get even messier.
You're probably right...
Urgh, I... I see. But could we not have solved\n
this all behind Atuy's back? Was that not a\n
possibility?
You mean your big plan?
Yes! If we follow my plan, this will all end up\n
a pleasant memory for Atuy, and nobody will\n
get hurt.
Nekone pridefully puffs out her chest with a\n
triumphant little chuckle.
Right, the plan... Take down the conmen ourselves,\n
disguise one of us as the guy, and go on the date.
And afterwards, we say the guy left on a long\n
journey, and let Atuy have her emotional farewell.
...The end in particular sounds more like the\n
synopsis of a cheap novel than an actual plan.
Yeah, that's not going to work.
Wh-Why do you say that? Are you not concerned\n
for Atuy!?
Sure I am... But why do you think neither Kuon\n
nor Rulutieh agreed to that plan?
Huh...?
Um... well...
Ahem, *cough, cough*...
Dear sister...? Rulutieh...?
Well, there are a lot of reasons.\n
The disguise part actually isn't so bad,\n
since she barely knows him.
Putting aside how we're supposed to even make\n
a disguise of him...
Of course it is not a bad idea.
...So which one of us is supposed to play the\n
part?
Well, that is...
I obviously can't do it because of my height.\n
And even if that wasn't a problem, there's\n
our physiques.
If I remember right, that guy was a full head\n
shorter than me. I think she'd notice that much.
Nekone glances at Kuon.
Awkwardness prevails as Kuon strains her neck\n
to look away.
Confused, Nekone then turns to Rulutieh.
Rulutieh begins to frantically shake her head\n
from side to side.
Yeah, that's what I thought.
From the looks of it, Kuon's a loner...\n
I mean, not a social butterfly.
She's talented, but if you asked her to play\n
a dashing and charming nobleman, she might...\n
have some trouble.
And judging by her reaction, she's probably\n
fully aware of this.
Rulutieh also would have problems with this,\n
just because of who she is by nature.
You went into a lot of detail, but theory and\n
practice are entirely different things.\n
It won't be as easy as that.
Not to mention this all centers around a love\n
affair. That'll be rough for the disguised person.
Um... Sorry, Nekone. I'd be glad to take an active\n
role, but if that plan were to fail...
N-No, you are not at fault here, dear sister.
When I think about it, she is a good actor.\n
I guess only when it involves trickery and deceit...?
...Did I hear something just now?
N-Nope. I didn't say anything.
A-Anyways, seeing as failure isn't really an\n
option, we can't go through with your plan,\n
Nekone.
Nngh...
And this is Atuy we're talking about. If she sees\n
an obstacle in her way, she'd just get fired up.
If we do something that makes her go nuts, there's\n
no way we'll be able to stop her.
Don't let it get you down. You're smart, but\n
you're still just a kid. It's OK to not get\n
adult matters.
*Thwack!*
Hnngah!?
That was supposed to be a good piece of advice!\n
Why did she kick me in the shins?
An open area beyond a deserted alley.
Atuy stands there, excitedly looking around\n
in anticipation.
It appears as though they are not here yet...
We loiter at a distance, far enough that neither\n
Atuy nor the ruffians would notice us.
Oh...
After a while, the men from the pub appear,\n
slowly surrounding Atuy.
They're here...
Didn't think they'd go straight for her, though.\n
They're making it way too obvious.
Thug
Hey there, beautiful. What're you doin' in a place\n
like this all by yourself?
Heh heh heh. How about you spend some time with\n
us, if you're free for the night?
Um... Wasn't that person over there the one\n
pretending to be the attendant?
What? You're right... Are these guys serious?\n
Do they even know what they're doing?
It sounded like they've used this plan for quite\n
a while now. I suppose they might be getting\n
careless.
So what Haku was saying was true, then...
...Wait, you didn't believe me until just now?
No, I did believe. Dear sister seemed to trust\n
you, so... there's no reason for me not to\n
trust you.
...Right.
But even Atuy would be able to catch on if these\n
guys are being this careless...
I kinda feel bad for her, but this is probably\n
for the best before she actually gets hurt...
But Atuy doesn't even seem to notice the thugs.\n
She keeps eagerly looking around.
Being ignored... does not seem to sit well\n
with her would-be assailants.
Oh, you waitin' out here for someone, little\n
missy?
Forget that guy. Come have some real fun with us.
Heh heh heh... yeah. We'll take you somewhere\n
real special.
Hmmm...? Oh, sorry, but do you have some\n
business with me?
...Tch. We're asking if you wanna go have some\n
fun with us.
Yeah, we'll show you a reeeaaal good time.
Ohhh, so that's what it was.\n
You were hitting on me...
Hee hee, guess I'm not such a lost cause after all.
Sorry, boys, but none of you are really my type.\n
Better luck next time, eh?
...Huh?
U-Um... Could it be that...
Atuy... has not noticed at all, has she?
Maybe she has so little interest in them that she\n
hasn't even bothered to look at them...
What's that? Don't get all high and mighty on us,\n
little girl.
We don't mind, really. We can have just as much\n
fun here too.
Thugs
Geheheheheh!
...You boys kinda smell, you know?
Your clothes are all so ugly, too.\n
You should pay more attention to how you\n
present yourselves.
...I bet you boys have trouble picking up girls,\n
don't you?
Wh-What!? You saying we don't get the ladies!?
'Course we get the girls! They're all over us!\n
All the time!!
Mm... Boys, deceiving yourself is never the\n
answer. You have to wake up and face reality.
That's none of your business!
You little bitch! You think you can get away with\n
sayin' that shit just 'cause you're kinda cute!?\n
Huh!?
Chalafun
Halt!! You scoundrels! Step away from Atuy!!
Oh, Chalafun...
Are you unhurt, Atuy? You must have been so\n
scared... but fear not. I have come for you.
...Hm? Scared...? But what's there to be scared of?
O-Oh... Y-Yes, your stoutheartedness is also\n
one of your many charms.
Hee hee! Oh, you flatter me, Chalafun.
Atuy seems completely oblivious, dithering and\n
blushing in girlish embarrassment.
U-Uh... And who do you think you are, you\n
little shit!?
Heh! You think you're so cool, pretty-boy!?
You think you can take us on with that skinny\n
little body?
Hah. The size of my body matters not. I shall stop\n
anyone who dares harm Atuy.
Whoa, looks like we got a tough guy! Guess we'll\n
have to beat you down first then!
Geheheheh! Lemme hear you scream!\n
What kind of sounds're you gonna make after\n
we're done with you!?
The ruffians raise their arms as they slowly\n
close in.
Atuy, stay back.
So, where are you going to take me tonight?\n
Ooh, I can hardly wait!
Uh, Atuy?
Quit ignoring us, bitch!
You think this is some kinda joke or somethin'?\n
Are we going to go watch a show?\n
Ooh, or maybe we could just go clothes shopping!
Er, well...
Hello!?
At least look at us, dammit!\n
LOOK AT US!!
...Oh, you were all still here?
Yeah! The whole damn time!
Oh dear. You know, if I were you, I'd go have\n
a bath instead of hanging around here.
You should learn from Chalafun!\n
Your faces may be hopeless, but you could be\n
a bit easier on the eyes.
...You're gonna pay for this, you asshole...
H-Hold it. I didn't say it, she did...!\n
N-Now begone, scum, or I shall teach you\n
a lesson!
Heh, fine by me! We'll beat you down until\n
you're never gettin' back up!
You fools. You shall regret your decision soon\n
enough.
...Ohhh, I get it now.\n
So that's how it is.
Huh?
Uh?
Sorry I didn't notice earlier, but you really\n
should've told me sooner if that was the\n
case.
I was wondering why you were still hanging out\n
around here, but you wanted some fun with me,\n
huh?
Uh... Yeah, that's what we said.\n
Did you really only realize now...?
This girl really is a nut, ain't she?
What are you saying, Atuy? Do not worry--\n
I shall dispose of them...
Well, let's have a go, shall we?
Fgyaaaarh!?
In an instant, with a thunderous sound, one of the\n
ruffians' face swells into a malformed shape.
It takes a second for me to realize it's from\n
Atuy just slapping the guy across the face.
Augh... Hrgghhh...
Of course, the amount of force was incredible\n
for just a slap.
His face looks practically caved in, and we can\n
see his teeth askew past the blood dripping out.
All the ruffians stand staring dumbly at Atuy,\n
as if they can't comprehend what just happened.
What, you're not even going to dodge?
Wh-Wh-Wh-What the FUCK was that for!?
Y-You bitch! We were taking it easy on you,\n
and you just take advantage of that!?
You'll pay for that!
W-Wait!
Two of the thugs swing at Atuy, but she stands\n
there nonchalantly, with no apparent intent to\n
move.
Gfaaaaaah...
Grbraaaah...
With two sharp sounds, the two men roll and skid\n
across the alley pavement until they hit the wall.
Wh-Who IS this girl!?
I-I-I-I'll fuckin' KILL you!
The remaining men come to, and quickly draw\n
daggers and shortswords.
Y-You idio--Hey, stop!
Don't think you're gettin' off easy for this!\n
I'll cut you to pieces!
It's too late to grovel and beg.\n
I'm gonna make your pretty face bleed all over\n
these stones.
Ooooh! Is that how we're going to play next?
Atuy grins in delight, and she opens up the\n
object she carries on her back.
Wh-What the...!?
The terrifying two-pronged spear unfolds to its\n
full size in her hands.
Th-This girl... She's fucking crazy...\n
Come on, do your thing!
Got it!
One of the ruffians opens up a cage at his waist.\n
Dangerous-looking bugs skitter out.
These're some of the most venomous bugs I could\n
find. One sting, and you'll be in agony 'til you\n
die.
You think we're a joke, huh...?\n
Well, now you've done it... You pissed us off!
Heh... Heh heh heh... It's too late to cry for\n
mercy now...
*Wriggle*
*Fwoop, fwoop, fwoop*
Kurarin leaves the top of Atuy's head, and\n
reaches out to nimbly snatch up the bugs.
*Crunch, munch, crunch*
WHAAAAT!?
Kurarin! What have I told you about eating things\n
off the ground? What if you make yourself sick?
*Jiggle, jiggle, jiggle*
*Fwoop, crunch, fwoop, crunch*
A-AHHHHHHH!? M-My bugs!\n
My precious bugs!
*Crunch, crunch, crunch*
S-Stop! Please, I'm begging you! Stop!
They're not venomous, I was just trying to scare\n
you--th-they just paralyze you for a little bit!!
PLEASE! STOOOOOP!!
Whaaaat...
But... I've barely even done anything yet.
Oh well. I guess this is my chance to show myself\n
off to Chalafun!
Keep your eyes on me now, OK, Chalafun?
Suddenly... a palpable menace descends on the\n
air in the area. It feels dark and oppressive.
...It's all Atuy. If she actually intends on\n
killing them, the pressure alone could stop a heart.
A simple thug has no chance of standing up\n
to something like this.
Eeeeeeeek! Please! Spare me!\n
I-I'll do anything!
I'm so sorry! I'll never do this again!\n
Please! Don't kill me!!
...Oh dear.
But I haven't even gotten to do anything yet.
Oh well. I guess I ought to finish this up, then.
The ruffians are frozen in terror. Atuy scoops\n
them both up by the necks, in the gap between\n
the prongs.
Hee hee, what a haul!
It almost looks like she's holding some kind of\n
giant skewer in her hands.
Oh, right...
Thanks for being so concerned about me, Chalafun.\n
I was sooo scared...
Atuy bashfully looks at the slim man, eyelashes\n
fluttering... the thugs hanging limply from the\n
spearhead above her.
Well, shall we get going, then? I'd still love\n
to know where you were planning on taking me...
Atuy eagerly sidles up, her voice still carrying\n
a cute lilt.
...Chalafun?
Atuy looks into the man's face.
Eep!?
The man suddenly breaks free from being frozen\n
with terror, staggers back, and falls on his rear.
...A pungent, sharp odor gradually becomes\n
noticeable.
AAAAAAAAHHHHHHHHHH!!
And with a scream of sheer terror, he scrambles\n
away on all fours, desperately trying to escape.
Atuy, clearly unsure of what just happened,\n
stands dumbfounded and forlorn.
Another!
Atuy empties her tenth glass in one go, and\n
demands another cup.
ANOTHER!
Maybe it's time to slow down there.\n
I think you've had enough already.
Those damn girls forced Atuy on me and left!\n
I'm no expert, but aren't girls supposed to\n
console each other for stuff like this?
Nooooo, I'm not drunk at allllll yet.
I'm pretty sure saying classic lines like that\n
is when you KNOW you're drunk.
Pfaaaah, this drink is what I live for.
*Sigh* Fine. I guess I can let her do as she\n
pleases for tonight, at least.
Mister! Can I get some tripe stew over here?
Cook
You got it.
...If things had gone my way...
If only things had gone my way, this would have\n
been the start of a beautiful love story...
I don't know why...
Why? Why...? Why does this happen, love?
Great. She's a complainer-drunk.\n
I didn't know she got this bad...
Her eyes droop heavily as she continues on\n
with her drunken rambling.
Something about this is still a little odd,\n
but now she's talking nonstop as she complains.
Honestly, she feels a lot more relatable than\n
usual, but I'm not sure that's a good thing.
Tell me something, love... Am I not attractive\n
as a girl?
Er... Well, I'd say nine out of ten guys would\n
probably find you attractive.
Really? You really mean that?
She's got curves in all the right places, so as\n
long as they don't find out how weird she gets...
Then why do they all run away from me?
Atuy leans towards me to get closer, intent on\n
hearing my answer.
I feel said curves pressing up against me slightly,\n
and I try to edge delicately back.
...Maybe they've got... specific tastes? You know,\n
like they like younger girls or something.
I'm pretty sure that terrifying presence would\n
scare off most guys...
I can't bring myself to tell her the truth, so\n
I fumble out some other reason.
I was pretty far from her, but I was about to\n
collapse on the spot after feeling that aura.
Hm? Wait, maybe that's why Kuon grabbed Rulutieh\n
and Nekone and ran off...
Now that I think about it, Nekone was on the\n
verge of tears. It must've really spooked her.
She may be a genius, but she's still just a kid.
Pfaaaaah!
To be honest, there's really nothing wrong with\n
her looks...
I see. Well, I suppose it wouldn't have worked out\n
if we didn't have any common interests...
You shouldn't worry about it. I know you'll\n
eventually find the right guy.
Really!? You think so?
Here you go, one tripe stew.
Yeah. *Munch, munch* Pfah, desh hoht...\n
You aren't planning on giving up already, are you?
I mean, that's why you came to the imperial capital\n
in the first place, isn't it?
Oh... you're right. If I give up because of one\n
little setback, I'll never find my true love.
Hey, some stewed clams and grilled fish please...?\n
There you go. There's always next time, right?
Here you go, an order of stewed clams and one\n
grilled fish.
I try to end the conversation there. I don't know\n
how much longer I can cheer her up.
You know what, love, you're right!\n
The past is the past. It's time to look to the\n
men of the future!
In that case, here's to new love.
Hee hee! I'm going to drink until I pass out\n
tonight. No more sad thoughts in the morning!
...What?
Hey, buddy, we're closing up soon, and your\n
lady's out cold. Can I ask you to pack it up\n
and head out?
Yeah, sorry for staying so long.
No worries. A pub is where people go to drink\n
and talk about their lives, eh? You come back\n
any time.
I think I will. Thanks for the food.
I pay our bill and pick up Atuy... who is\n
completely conked out.
The cold morning air feels good after a night of\n
drinking.
Looks like she's even a handful for you, Kurarin.
I can feel the soft weight of Atuy on my back\n
and arms as I carry her. I talk to Kurarin for\n
a distraction.
Kurarin extends a tentacle and pats my shoulder,\n
as though to acknowledge my hard work.
You know what, man?\n
You're all right.
Its tentacles waggle airily, as if to say\n
"all in a day's work."
Nn...
Atuy's grip on me suddenly tightens.
I feel the back of my shirt dampening a little\n
as she buries her face in it.
...Guess it's not that easy to get over.
Ah, well. For her sake, I'll deal with it, just\n
this once.
Hey, Rulutieh, how about we make a hotpot for\n
tonight?
Ah, there should be some fish in season,\n
actually...
They have plenty of fat this time of year.\n
They should be delicious.
Fish, huh... Mm. That does sound good.\n
All right! Fish it is.
If you need a fishmonger, I know of one I can\n
recommend.
The one across the street over there makes a\n
point of keeping very fresh catches on offer.
Well, if it's got a glowing review from Nekone,\n
I think that seals it.
Oh, yeah. Haku, any requests for food?
No, I've been wondering about something for a\n
while. Why has Rulutieh been cooking our meals?
The inn has kitchen staff. She doesn't have to\n
inconvenience herself like this.
A-Ah, actually, Sir Haku... I'm doing this at\n
my own request.
Really?
Y-Yes! I... I want to be of help somehow.\n
And I'm... good with cooking, so...
Lady Kuon and the Hakurokaku staff are\n
graciously allowing me to use the kitchens...
Um. Is it... an inconvenience?
O-Oh, no, not at all. Your cooking is always\n
excellent, Rulutieh.
I wouldn't mind if you cooked for me every day\n
from here on out, in fact.
...Dear sister.
Yes, he probably doesn't even realize what\n
that just sounded like, I think.
...What are you two talking about?
Clear the road! Make a path, and make it wide!
Hm?
A loud voice suddenly cuts the conversation\n
short.
The crowd filling the marketplace splits,\n
moving to the sides of the street...
Wh-What's going on?
If I recall correctly, today should be the day\n
for it.
Today? What's happening today?
Well... Here they come.
At the far end of the market street, a woptor-\n
mounted group marches forward.
Oh, it's Oshtor...?\n
Or... Huh? Something seems off...
Mm, they're flying a different banner.\n
Unless I've missed my guess, that must be...
Before long, the mounted figures draw near\n
enough to be identified more easily.
Each soldier is heavily armed and armored, and\n
assembled as they are, they project an\n
imposing air.
One man in particularly fine armor leads his\n
fellows, standing out from the group.
More than likely, he's the commanding officer.
The Imperial Guard of the Left, Lord Mikazuchi.
Mikazuchi? Then...
So he's Oshtor's counterpart?
Recalling rumors I'd heard before, I voice my\n
confusion aloud.
Yes. Together with my dear b--with Lord Oshtor,\n
he is famed as one of the Twin Shields of\n
Yamato.
Today is his day to make his symbolic patrol\n
of the city streets.
An officer equal to Oshtor, huh...
I guess the pomp and circumstance is\n
understandable, then. Still...
I take a quick look around.
The market street, previously bustling, has\n
fallen completely silent.
When Oshtor showed up on his patrol, the crowd\n
had cheered him. With this guy, though...
Everyone seems to avert their eyes.
Probably because of... that.
That terrifying aura he gives off.
While Oshtor's demeanor seems to embrace people,\n
this guy's feels more like a brandished sword.
It feels like I might cut myself if I get too\n
close... before I even get a chance to say\n
anything.
The procession continues, and before long,\n
the soldiers pass in front of our group.
Abruptly, Mikazuchi calls a halt and turns to\n
stare directly at us.
Hrk.
His woptor comes to a stop, and his piercing\n
eyes bore straight through us.
Heh.
He grins wolfishly, like a hunter finally\n
catching up to its prey.
Nekone hurriedly ducks behind me, hiding.
Wh--Hey!
The movement attracts his gaze, and inevitably,\n
those awful eyes settle on me.
Wha... What's this terrible sense of... dread...?
Letting his stare linger on me, Mikazuchi's\n
smile widens--
Heh... Ah heh heh heh.
Then, seemingly satisfied, he sets his woptor\n
moving again.
Heh heh.
With his unsettling laughter echoing through\n
the silent street, Mikazuchi leads his troops\n
onward.
The market crowd breathes a collective sigh\n
of relief.
Phew...
Nekone, too, finally seems to relax and\n
steps out from behind me.
So that was Mikazuchi, Imperial Guard of the\n
Left...
He seems like a scary person...
Yeah. It was enough to frighten this little\n
squirt, that's for sure.
Ow!
Little brat. N-Not the shins...
Hey, Haku. Wanna go on a shopping trip with me?
The door suddenly opens, admitting Kuon into\n
my room, where I've been relaxing with some\n
tea.
I don't mind, but what are you planning on\n
buying?
I was going to see if the produce market has\n
the fresh fruit I need to make bath oils.
Bath oils?
You haven't heard of it? You put it in the\n
water, and it gives off a fragrance and soothes\n
your skin.
One of the inn workers told me I could use\n
some in the baths, so I thought I'd make my\n
own.
Oh, she must be talking about a bath additive.
Hm? Additives, though...? Is it really OK to\n
put those in a communal bath?
The face of the inn's proprietress flashes\n
through my mind.
...She's sure it won't be a big deal... right?
I need a lot of fruit to make a full batch,\n
though, which is where you come in.
Oh. You just want me to be a pack mule, in\n
other words.
Do you mind?
Eh. I'm not busy, so not really.
All right! Then let's go.
Kuon smiles and grabs ahold of my hand.
Whoa, easy. Don't pull me so roughly.
And as usual, "bath" is the magic word to\n
unlock Kuon's enthusiasm...
I haven't been to the market here before.\n
It's full of all kinds of fresh produce,\n
fruit and otherwise.
Fragrances both sweet and sour attack my nose\n
from every single booth.
Just looking at the colorful array of fruit\n
laid out for sale is enough to excite and\n
tantalize...
To be expected of the center of the empire,\n
I guess. There's stuff here I haven't even\n
heard of.
With a pensive look on her face, Kuon inspects\n
a colorful, prickly-looking fruit...
Even something like this, huh? So much I've\n
never seen before...
That's a weird one. You're gonna make bath oil\n
out of that?
Ahahaha, not this one, but the variety is...\n
intimidating.
I know! Excuse me, could I get some of these\n
cut up for samples?
The shop owner complies with her request, and\n
Kuon takes in the fragrance of each new\n
slice...
This one's pretty sweet. I think Nekone would\n
appreciate it.
Hmhm. It is the kind of thing Nekone would\n
like, isn't it?
This one... Eh, a bit grassy. Not exactly ideal\n
for a scent you want to put in your bath...
I pluck what seems to be a citrus fruit from\n
the display and inhale deeply.
So you like those types of fruit, huh.
Eh, it's not that I have a preference, they\n
just... seem like the kind you'd put in your\n
bath?
I can see it easily, if I close my eyes.\n
Citrus fruits floating in a bathtub.
Hmhm, I see... And I agree.
I'll take these red ones, those little orange\n
ones, and... that long one, there.
That one too? It's got a pretty weird smell,\n
though.
Well, we don't want the sweetness to be\n
overpowering. The whole thing wouldn't come\n
together, otherwise.
Is that how it works...?
Yes, that's how it works.
Kuon happily plucks another fruit from the\n
stand.
She really does love her baths, doesn't she...?
I think we might've bought a little too much,\n
They're all useful, so it should be fine\n
either way.
Many pieces of fruit bob and float in the\n
bathwater that evening, and they suffuse it\n
with their scents.
We bought quite a bit to make the oils, so this\n
will do until Kuon finishes refining it...
It seems to be a hit with the rest of the inn,\n
for more than one guest has emerged smelling\n
of fruit.
I suppose I should go take one, too...
Yeah. I think I'll go take a nice, long bath\n
and munch on a piece of fruit.
A trip to the mountains? Tomorrow?\n
This is kind of sudden...
Rulutieh said she was going to go harvest the\n
wild vegetables in season. I thought I'd tag\n
along.
Oh, for fresh ingredients and all that stuff?
Yes... Um... These vegetables taste best when\n
they're in season... And they're good for your\n
health...
I'm running low on herbs as well. A perfect\n
opportunity, I'd say. You're coming too, right,\n
Haku?
...Wouldn't it be faster to just go buy some\n
from the market...?
My suggestion is immediately denied.
Hahh... guh... phew...
We've been climbing since before the sun even\n
rose, and it's noon! How high do they intend on\n
going...?
I NEED a break, but since Rulutieh and Nekone\n
aren't even out of breath, I keep my mouth shut.
Um, everyone... how about we use this area...\n
as the focal point for our search...?
Rulutieh stops in an open area and calls out\n
to everyone.
Are you sure we do not have to go any farther?
Yes... I believe that... we'll be able to find\n
much around here...
I'll leave this area to all of you, then. I'm\n
going a bit higher up to find the herbs I need.
If you are still going, dear sister, I can\n
accompany...
Sorry, but those cliffs can be treacherous.\n
I'd prefer it if you looked for my herbs around\n
here.
Understood. If that is what you wish, dear sister.
She's so obedient whenever it's Kuon asking\n
something.
As I think to myself, Kuon vanishes deeper into\n
the forest.
I don't know much about the mountains, so I'll\n
stick with you, Neko.
You can help me look for herbs, then.
Lovely! So will this do?
Ugh, that smells! N-No, that is not any herb of\n
note.
How about this then?
Wh--That one is extremely poisonous! Please\n
dispose of it, and quickly!
Nekone and Atuy seem to be having fun as they\n
chat together, poking through the roots and\n
weeds.
I'll remain on watch here, then. I'm sure there's\n
no real danger about, but better safe than sorry.
He watches Nekone and Atuy leave, smiling amicably\n
at me as he makes his declaration.
This could have been a perfect opportunity for\n
you to take the initiative with her, you know...
...Pardon?
Never mind. Well, I guess watch duty is yours,\n
then.
Ah, well. I guess you can't expect people to\n
change that easily.
Welp, what should I do? I don't even have a clue\n
what I'm supposed to be looking for here.
U-Um... Sir Haku... If you'd like, would you\n
accompany me...?
Rulutieh asks me very hesitantly.
Yeah, sure. I wouldn't know what to pick if I went\n
off on my own. Just tell me what to do, Rulutieh.
O-Oh no. I wouldn't dare... I just wanted to go\n
searching with you... That's all...
She's as shy as ever. She doesn't have to be so\n
reserved around me all the time...
Well, in any case, I'd appreciate it if you could\n
teach me a thing or two about the mountains.
Y-Yes... Well, there are many things... that\n
can only be found during this season.
I carry a basket along with me as I amble along\n
behind Rulutieh.
Rulutieh's vast knowledge on the subject helps us\n
find various interesting plants, one after\n
another.
And on these trees... you can find mushrooms with\n
a very distinct texture...
Hmmm, lemme see...
It's this one, here... Eep!
Hm? What's the matter?
I stand closer, trying to get a look at the\n
mushrooms, but she yelps as I look over her\n
shoulder.
N-No... you're... just a little... close...
She's talking so quietly. I can't really hear\n
what she's saying.
Uh... It's... It's nothing...
OK...? Hm, yeah, you're right. These mushrooms\n
do feel really springy.
If you steam them... they get a firm, almost\n
crunchy texture. It's very good...
Really... Huh?
When I try to pluck one of the mushrooms, it\n
instantly crumbles in my hands.
Oh... If you grab it directly like that, it'll\n
fall apart... You have to break it off at the\n
stem...
Like this?
I copy Rulutieh's motions, plucking it directly\n
from the base. This time, it remains intact.
Yes... That was very good...
I see... Not so bad once you get the hang of it.\n
Here, you can put yours in this basket too.
O-Oh... Thank you...
Might've been a lot easier if we had taken\n
Cocopo with us...
I'm sorry... but if we had brought Cocopo here...
What? Something bad?
I'm sure that everything in the vicinity that's\n
even remotely edible... would be eaten...
Ah... I see.
Well, sounds like a good enough reason to me.
We continue to talk for a while, until...
Oh... This tree...
Rulutieh stops in front of a tree, and puts her\n
hand to its trunk.
Something up with it?
It looks like just an ordinary tree.\n
No weird nuts, or mushrooms, or tuber-based vines.
Rulutieh pinches a reddish-brown substance off\n
the trunk, and touches it to her tongue.
Hee hee...
Her face brightens into a fond, childlike smile,\n
as though remembering something nostalgic.
I follow her lead--taking a pinch of the substance\n
and throwing it into my mouth.
...That's sweet.
It has a powerful flavor of sap, with a subtle\n
sweetness that seems to fill my mouth.
When I was little... my elder brothers and sisters\n
would take me out to play... and we'd eat these...
Sweets were rather expensive, so we would often\n
have this for a snack instead...
Ah, she was remembering her childhood.
Around us are several more of the same trees,\n
growing in a cluster.
I had no idea this tree grew in these lands as\n
well... It feels so nostalgic...
A little taste of home, I guess.
I wonder... how they're all doing right now...
She looks up at the tree, a strange expression\n
on her face. She must be thinking of her\n
siblings...
Yeah, I heard you have a lot of brothers and\n
sisters.
Yes... All told, we make fourteen.
That sounds... hectic. Bet you were never bored\n
as a kid, at least.
Hee... Yes. My brothers... and sisters... would\n
often visit me to play...
Visit...? Right, she spent a lot of her childhood\n
sick in bed...
They must have been good siblings to you, then.
Yes... They were very kind... I'm proud to call\n
them my brothers and sisters.
When I had fallen ill, and was bedridden... They\n
would often bring me this very sap...
As we talk, I realize the sap in my mouth has\n
melted away.
If this sap was just a little more mild, it\n
could've been used as a sweetener.
Sugar and honey can get expensive.\n
If I collect some of this and heat--Ah, but the\n
flavor's too strong...
It has a slight bitterness, and it's a bit sharp.\n
Adding it to drinks and food would ruin the\n
flavor.
I wonder if there's something I can do about it...
Is... something the matter?
Eh, nothing. Just wondering whether we could make\n
some kind of sweetener with this stuff.
A... sweetener?
Yeah. I was thinking if we gather some, we could\n
try heating it to make it a little sweeter.
But it's got a pretty distinct flavor to it,\n
so it might not be that easy.
Oh...

like?

Mysterious, maybe...?\n
Enigmatic?
She's beautiful, that much is certain.
An enigmatic beauty, eh...
Kuon seems suspicious for some reason.
Do you want to come with me?
Eh? Come with you?
I try inviting Kuon along, but her response\n
seems... hesitant.
Are you sure that's OK?
Says here to "extend invitations" to my\n
friends. It oughta be fine.
Ah, so it does. All right, I'm curious now.\n
I'll come with you.
I've been wanting to meet the owner of this\n
place, but I haven't had a chance yet.
And the "fine libations and food" part has my\n
attention.
Of course that's what you're actually after...
With Kuon in tow, I head for the top floor,\n
where we find wide, open windows looking over\n
the imperial capital.
The cityscape stretches all the way out to the\n
horizon.
Wow, that's some view. I never imagined THIS\n
was hiding on the top floor...
Kuon's eyes fix on the distant horizon,\n
overlooking the city below.
...are you sure this is the right place?\n
It's empty...
No, this isn't it. Hold on.
Huh?
I wink at Kuon and walk up to the mechanism\n
locking the stairs--and find a surprise.
Worker
If I recall correctly, this goes here...\n
No... was it here?
One of the Hakurokaku's workers crouches by\n
the mechanism, talking to herself obliviously.
Then this piece goes... vertically?\n
No, that is not it either...
She seems to be struggling with the puzzle.
This foul contrivance. Have I made a mistake?\n
Such carelessness is inexcusable...
Perhaps the workings are merely loose?\n
Should I push harder...?
H-Hey, hold on! Wait!
I manage to stop her just before she throws\n
her full weight against the device.
A-Augh!! Who goes th--\n
I-I mean, can I help you, sir?
Hm? That pattern... isn't that...?
Hyah!?
The worker's eyes fall on Kuon, and she makes\n
an odd noise.
Pardon us. We were invited up here by the\n
owner, and we'd like to go up.
The owner?
Truly? Karu--? I mean, ah. Yes, sir. Of course.
Go... up? Haku, aren't we on the top floor?
You'd think that...
There's a gimmick to it. A hidden stairway\n
appears if you work this puzzle correctly.
Aha, I knew it! An old clockwork device...
Kuon traces the intricate workings with a\n
finger, her eyes positively shining.
Ah, this takes me back. I had no idea this\n
country had devices like this.
You have something like this in your\n
homeland?
Uh-huh.\n
My mother, see...
She begins to shift the pieces of the puzzle\n
as she talks.
She collected objects like this. It always seemed\n
out-of-character for her, but...
There we are. This one goes here, that one\n
like this, and then...
The puzzle-like patterns of the lock snap\n
together, and the stairs descend from above.
There we go. Impressed yet?
I solved it too, you know.
Just... not as quickly as you did.
Could I ask you a question, miss?
I am--that is to say, ah--Housekeeping!\n
I'm very busy. Doing housekeeping. Yes.
The worker abruptly turns to begin wiping a\n
perfectly clean section of the wall.
Have we... met before, miss?
Hah! See there! Stubborn stains in the\n
paneling!
As Kuon approaches her to get a better look at\n
her face, she crouches quickly to the floor.
Miss?
Next to us, the stairs slide out and unfold,\n
unlocked by Kuon's handiwork.
There's our way up. If you'll excuse us,\n
ma'am.
Y-Yes, sir! Please do not mind me!
With that, the worker returns to her wiping,\n
her nose inches from the floor.
Come on, Kuon. She's got a job to do.
A-All right...
She'll be fine. I think she's just embarrassed\n
we saw her screw up the lock.
I whisper my suspicion to Kuon, and she lets\n
it go, moving to follow me up.
As we emerge into the hidden attic, the same\n
incense as before assaults my nose.
My eyes take a moment to adjust to the dark.\n
I scan the familiar room...
But the enigmatic woman from before is nowhere\n
to be found.
...Maybe she's out?
Hm. She has good taste.
Kuon breathes her admiration as she takes in\n
the scenery.
This atmosphere, though...\n
It reminds me of--
Hmhm...
Playful, satisfied laughter, like a chess master\n
admiring a move, echoes in the dark.
Kuon flinches, freezing in place.
I look over my shoulder, and there sits the\n
Hakurokaku's owner, as if she'd been there the\n
entire time.
I never expected such a compliment from YOU\n
of all people, dear.
Kuon turns, still rooted in place, and I swear\n
I can hear her slowly grinding to face her.
I'm pleased to see your tastes have developed\n
quite nicely. It's been some time, hasn't it?
I'm happy to see you well, Kuon.
M-Mothe--!?
Kuon doesn't get to finish the word.
A sudden, palpable air of intimidation fills\n
the room, blade-like and sharp.
If looks could kill, I might have just seen\n
Kuon's head severed from her shoulders.
Could it just be my imagination...?
U-Urk...
Or maybe Kuon felt it too. Her hand goes to\n
her neck, as though the hair on it had stood\n
up.
What was that, now? Did you say something,\n
my dear?
A-Ah, no--nothing, dear sister. Nothing.
Huh? You know her?
They definitely don't seem like simple\n
acquaintances, that much is for sure.
Sh-She's, ah... one of the m--Sisters!!\n
Sisters, who raised me.
A few years back, she said she was going on\n
a trip and disappeared, but now...
Fate weaves such an... interesting tapestry,\n
wouldn't you agree?
She smiles self-assuredly, but it only seems\n
to make Kuon more uncomfortable.
Karulau must have some kind of leverage over\n
her.
Please have a seat, won't you? You're guests,\n
after all. Welcome to my room.
We each find seats across the table from\n
Karulau. Something feels off.
What are we supposed to do now?
The "fine libations and food" are nowhere in\n
sight.
Karulau simply sits, eyes resting on Kuon,\n
smiling oddly.
Kuon avoids her stare, stiff and awkward.
This feels more like an interview for a job\n
than a feast...
Hey, Kuon.
I whisper to Kuon, who remains frozen in...\n
terror?
She's your family, right? Don't you have,\n
y'know, things to talk about?
I've never seen her like this...
Don't tell me you're clamming up?
W-Well...
A-Among my mothers, you see--Karulau is the one\n
I most admire, the one I emulate...
Mothers?
The unusual plural catches me off-guard.\n
Huh...
Karulau is difficult to figure out. She has\n
a noble bearing, a knowing smile...
But... if there's one thing I know for certain,\n
she's important to Kuon somehow.
Oh? Don't be whispering to each other, now.\n
What's the big secret?
Anything you care to let me in on?
She smiles teasingly, and Kuon decides to\n
break her silence.
You haven't asked me why I came here to\n
Yamato...
I see. You want me to be curious about that,\n
do you?
Her smile renders Kuon silent again.
It's like a sulking child being seen through\n
by her parents. So strange...
Just as the "conversation" seems to be going\n
nowhere, I start to speak up, when--
Lord and ladies, forgive m--Ah, I mean...\n
Thank you for waiting, honored guests.
That strange worker returns with cups balanced\n
on a tray.
Thank you two for waiting.\n
Now, shall we have a toast?
The cups she places down are masterworks,\n
even to an amateur's eyes.
I-I'm very sorry! I'll clean it right away...
But her movement is strange and stilted.\n
She's clearly used to this work, but it's\n
oddly awkward...
Be that as it may, why is she serving us while\n
looking away...? Some weird local etiquette\n
thing?
...Hmm?
Kuon seems to have noticed something.
Wait... You can't be...!?
Kuon bolts up in a swift motion and stares the\n
housekeeper in her face.
...Mother... Touka!?
Hyah!? N-No! I have no idea who you could be\n
referring to! At all! I am a... a mere\n
housekeeper...
I knew there was something strange about you...\n
But it's really you, mother.
Ughhh... I-I am not this... this Touka...\n
No...
If you've been so close to me all this time,\n
why didn't you tell me, I wonder!?
Touka, why don't you stop serving us?\n
Our beloved sister is with us now.
Nngh...
Touka the housekeeper... well, the lady in a\n
housekeeper's uniform... finally turns her\n
face to us.
She seems different from the owner, but she's\n
definitely pretty. Kind of masculine, but\n
charming...
...Karulau...
Something wrong?
You knew Kuon would be coming up here, and\n
told me nothing of it?
Oh, but I'm sure I did, didn't I? I said that\n
I would invite someone important; someone also\n
familiar to you.
...I will listen to your excuses later.
The housekeeper quakes with anger for a moment\n
more, but finally turns her calm face to Kuon.
It's been a long time... How have you been?\n
You have grown since the last time I saw\n
you...
Touka hugs Kuon with both arms.
I wanted to say something to you on the first\n
day, but I lost my chance. My apologies...
Her gestures are a bit uncouth and awkward, but\n
I can feel her sincere love for Kuon in them.
Mother...
Kuon seems a bit embarrassed by this, but\n
there's a genuine and deep happiness there,\n
too.
The owner watches them peaceably, eyes\n
half-lidded.
That face says... "My girl can be so defiant\n
to me. But she can act so sweet with other\n
people."
Did you say something just now?
...No, miss, nothing.
Touka, don't forget that we're entertaining\n
a guest.
Hm. You are right.
Touka gently releases Kuon, and politely turns\n
her attention to me.
Allow me to introduce myself. I am Touka,\n
a worker of the Hakurokaku Inn. A pleasure\n
to meet you, sir.
You realize we are the only ones here?\n
You don't have to worry so much about showing\n
your true self.
Currently, I am nothing more than a worker of\n
the Hakurokaku Inn. No more, no less... ma'am.
Karulau smiles wryly as Touka says this, and\n
gives a small sigh in response.
It feels like I'm missing something here...\n
but probably best I don't pry into it.
Pleasure to meet you. I guess both Kuon and I\n
have been in your care, then.
I'm pretty sure I'm the one who's been taking\n
care of you, Haku.
Kuon interjects for some reason, but she seems\n
a little more childlike and playful than usual.
Likewise... Please look after Kuon for me, if\n
you would.
She bows deeply as she speaks. She seems more\n
like a mononofu than a simple worker.
Now then, shall we resume our little party?
Let us start with a drink.
The cup she passes to me is filled to the brim\n
with liquor.
Hm...
It looks like the same kind I've drank here\n
before, but the scent is more powerful.
Looks like you still have some excellent\n
drinks in your collection...
Oh, but you haven't even tasted it yet.\n
Good drinks aren't meant to be enjoyed on\n
aroma alone, you know?
She chuckles a bit as she speaks.
If I may, I'd like to pour you a cup as\n
well.
I would gladly accept it.
You will be drinking with us, of course, Touka?
Then I will have... I mean, if you would be\n
so kind, dear guest.
Touka raises her cup as she says this.\n
But, uh, that...
That's... a little big for drinking. It's more\n
like a full-on tankard.
I guess some people enjoy it in big cups as\n
well. She must really be a heavy drinker...
If you're OK with it straight from the\n
bottle, I can...
Oh, no, you misunderstand me... Dear guest,\n
I wish to have some tea.
My, my. Bringing down the mood a bit, aren't\n
you, Touka? This is a party, is it not, dear\n
guest?
Karulau's glance flickers toward me, as though\n
pressing me to go along with what she's\n
saying.
Oh, you don't have to be so modest. Here,\n
drink as much as you want.
Oh, no, I am in the middle of work at the\n
moment, and--Stop stop stop stop stop stop stop!
*Sigh*...
Haku, what about me?
I'll pour your drink for you.
Huh? Mo... ah, Sister?
W-Wait, Karulau. I should be the one to--
Allow me this, and we can call our debt\n
settled.
Wha--
B-But...
You won't remain a child forever. I suppose\n
it is time I permit you to drink. So, do you\n
accept?
Uh...
Hmhm... I never thought I'd see the day.\n
I suppose time flies.
Urgh... If only I had not owed her that\n
favor...
Let us have a toast, then, to celebrate this\n
happy occasion.
To this wonderful day, and this wonderful place.\n
To celebrate these wonderful people.
Cheers.
...Cheers.
We all begin to drink, and the mood soon\n
brightens.
Guess all her talk of rare liquors and foreign\n
delicacies wasn't for nothing.
The dishes being brought out are all things\n
I've never seen before, with distinct and\n
delicious tastes.
They try to explain the origin of each new\n
treat, but I'm way too busy stuffing my face\n
to listen.
What's more--
...Amazing!
I can't help but exclaim as I taste the liquors.\n
Every single one they bring out is fantastic.
It gives me a certain pride as the inn's\n
proprietor to see you enjoying yourself so\n
much.
This one's different. An Ushka, maybe? It's got\n
a strong flavor, but a smooth aftertaste.
Yes, that is something we imported from our\n
homeland. I thought it would go well with the\n
food.
Oh, definitely.
It is from a seller we often go to, but it\n
seems much rarer these days. Perhaps its\n
popularity is waning...
I am quite proud of myself for being able to\n
find some at all.
She fills my cup as she tells me all this.
This really is paradise... I guess sometimes\n
what you want most is right in front of you.\n
Or above you, I guess?
I slip into a nice buzz, feeling my thoughts\n
beginning to slow and relax.
This mead... I remember the taste. It's the\n
same one I drank behind your backs when I was\n
little.
I've been looking for this for a while now,\n
but I could never find it... This is so\n
nostalgic...
Of course you wouldn't find it. This is a\n
particularly special one... It was homemade\n
by her.
Then... this is...
I saved it for this very day. Make sure to\n
savor the taste, now.
...I will.
Touka watches Kuon for a while.
Pensive and quiet, she seems for a moment like\n
she's about to cry, but she takes a long drink\n
instead.
*Gulp, gulp, gulp.*
You sure you don't want seconds?
...I will have some, if you please.
I decide not to ask any questions, and pour\n
more liquor into her mug.
*Gulp, gulp*
Mother?
Why don't you leave her be for now? Sometimes\n
one simply wishes to lose themselves in liquor.
R-Right...
Have you tried any of these? This is made by\n
fermenting freshwater fish in a barrel of salt\n
for years.
It can be a bit strong, but I confess it has\n
me quite hooked.
I'll have some then...
Oh, heshuko! I loved this when I was little,\n
but they never allowed me to eat much of it.
I couldn't have you eating away at all the\n
snacks I intended to have on my drinking\n
nights.
Can you imagine the heartbreak when I found\n
the barrel I had preserved for years, emptied\n
by you?
A-Ahahaha... I do recall... something like that\n
happening?
Kuon seems to be feeling a little more\n
comfortable now.
By the way, Mothe--Sister.
And why all the sudden formality?
May I ask why you are running an inn in a place\n
like this?
Was that really the question you wanted to\n
ask me?
Er...
Kuon averts her eyes a little guiltily, as\n
Karulau fixes her gaze directly on her.
Well, let's see...
Karulau sets down her cup as she pauses, trying\n
to remember how everything began.
Kuon, what did you think when you first saw\n
this country of Yamato?
Um... I hate to admit it, but it's much larger\n
and more developed than our homeland.
Although... That's not to say our land is\n
inferior...
Indeed, the technological advances of this\n
country were quite astounding.
Their culture, technology, average wealth,\n
roadways, irrigation... and, of course, the\n
peaceful rule of their Mikado.
Yes, I do agree that there are many good\n
points about this country...
Kuon answers promptly, as though called upon\n
in a classroom.
After we saw this, we decided to change our\n
plans for a brief visit... and instead reside\n
here a while longer.
You see, we wished to learn more of this\n
country.
Are you saying that you prefer living here to\n
our home...?
Hmhm... You know, I find it's better to wait\n
until one finishes talking before responding,\n
dear.
...So, as we grew accustomed to our lives\n
here, we began to understand the full scale\n
of Yamato.
...As well as its shortcomings.
Shortcomings?
Yes. And the most egregious one\n
of all... was its baths.
...Huh?
Oh!
It seems this country's custom is to take\n
steam baths. There are few places to immerse\n
oneself in a tub...
Some inns out there have water baths, but they\n
are few and far between... and expensive, at\n
that.
Yes, that's true!
I thought I was misunderstanding this whole\n
conversation, but Kuon nods in fierce\n
agreement.
I heard steam baths are common in this region\n
because of past water shortages. A relic of\n
its past...
You could just call it cultural differences...\n
but I never imagined I would miss water bathing\n
so much.
I see, so that's why...
Yes. Thus the Hakurokaku Inn came to be.\n
I couldn't stand it anymore, so I decided to\n
fix the problem myself.
Thanks to that, I can now enjoy a nice bath\n
in hot water every single day.
Amazing... That certainly is the mo--\n
the sister I know.
...Wait, wait, wait. That conversation just now\n
made sense to you!?
Oh? Were my baths not to your liking? I pride\n
myself on having the best in the imperial\n
capital.
Look, I kind of see where you're coming from...
But would anyone really go to the trouble of\n
building an inn just so they can bathe the\n
way they want...?
That said, it wasn't easy, you know?
Karulau swirls the liquid in her cup as she\n
stares off into the distance wistfully.
Water baths require a plentiful water source...\n
but it seems the imperial capital lacks\n
aquifers.
And using water from the irrigation system\n
would cost a small fortune.
Oh, right. Water is usually free, but if you\n
use it for business, it's pretty heavily\n
taxed...
Hm, guess that's why steam baths are more\n
common in these areas...
Yes. It's quite well thought out, really.
With little option, we looked for a nearby\n
water source, and asked a friend to...\n
nudge it closer.
Oh, you are one to talk of its difficulty.\n
It was I who had done most of the work.
I was the one who brought Ul--ahem. "Her" here\n
in the first place. You did nothing at all\n
then, Karulau.
Wait... you don't mean you brought Mother\n
Ulthury all the way here just to move the\n
water source?
Karulau avoids the question as she takes\n
another sip from her cup.
But... But if she was brought here.. that means\n
the Oruyankuru came all the way here just\n
for...
Oh, it wasn't that bad. I wished to give her\n
a little vacation. She had been all cooped up\n
with work.
If your intentions were so kind, I suggest you\n
actually tell her beforehand next time.
I thought you told her of your plight!\n
Can you imagine my panic, realizing we were\n
spiriting her away unawares?
Oh, I had no choice! That was the only way\n
that girl would ever get to sneak out and\n
enjoy some nice baths.
Then that time she was reported as missing for\n
a while... Was that...?
The whole place was in an uproar when that\n
happened, you know...
My deepest apologies. Oh, such carelessness\n
is inexcusable...
However, I did send back a message immediately.\n
I am sure all the panic subsided after that.
I didn't hear a word of it.\n
I was so worried...
It isn't something easily explained to a child,\n
I suppose. That's probably why they never told\n
you.
Well... I suppose it couldn't be helped...
Ahem. There were those ruffians that forced\n
their way in and demanded a "protection" fee.\n
Very troublesome.
Oh, but I did offer to take care of them\n
myself. Tell me, who was the one who locked\n
me up then?
How could I not? Those scoundrels destroyed\n
your baths. You would have had their blood\n
rain from the skies!
All my efforts to settle matters quietly and\n
discreetly would have been ruined.
Quite the accusation. I only intended on giving\n
them a little... talking-to.
So you settled it quietly and discreetly,\n
did you now...?
Ngh... If you would say something, then say it.
Kuon, do you know what happened after that?
If I recall, I believe I heard someone\n
spending the rest of the night fleeing the\n
authorities...?
W-Wait! Nevermind! Kuon, do not listen to a\n
word Karulau says. It was nothing!\n
Nothing happened.
A-Ahahaha... I guess neither of you have really\n
changed.
I don't really get what they're talking about,\n
but I guess I shouldn't interrupt.
I wonder what the relationship is between\n
these two.
An innkeeper and a worker... but they don't\n
feel like a boss and an employee, nor just\n
friends.
How should I put this...
So... Mother, why are you just a worker here?
Kuon asks, just as the same question enters my\n
mind.
That is...
I would've thought that you would prefer to\n
take a job more like... a bodyguard, or\n
somesuch.
Ahem, that is, if I am to give a proper answer...\n
I would say it is training to become a better\n
woman.
Oh?
...I did work as a bodyguard here, for a time.
However, the imperial capital is mostly free\n
of crime, and few would antagonize the\n
Hakurokaku Inn.
The peace continued, and I had no further\n
duties. All I could do was wait for something\n
to happen.
Those ruffians must have had quite the\n
terrifying experience. Poor things, indeed.
Nngh... You are one to talk.
Hmhm.
So... it was rather hard for me to sit around\n
and do nothing whilst those around me were\n
hard at work.
...How?
Hm?
Oh, uh, nothing.
Why would it be hard to just sit around and do\n
nothing all day...?
Haku...
What? Why are you looking at me like that?
One day, I could stand it no longer.\n
I decided to work in this manner until my true\n
skills would be needed.
If you'd ask me, I would have preferred that\n
you stay put and keep quiet.
What are you saying? Those that do not work\n
do not deserve to eat. Do you not remember our\n
lord's words?
I suppose you're right. He was fond of saying\n
that, wasn't he...
Karulau smiles despite herself, as if dwelling\n
on some distant memory.
Those that do not work do not deserve to eat...\n
Ugh. What a horrible thing to say.
I dunno if I'd ever get along with this "Lord"\n
of theirs...
Just as I pour another drink, a new dish\n
arrives as if it had been waiting for the\n
perfect moment.
The food piled on top of the plate looks\n
vaguely familiar to me.
Oh... Mororo! And so many...!
Kuon's eyes glimmer with excitement at the\n
I thought you might be missing them by now,\n
so I obtained some just for you.
We wished you to have them, Kuon. They are\n
quite rare in this land... I am sure it has\n
been a while.
Yes, it has!
Kuon answers distractedly, taking one of the\n
mororo and biting eagerly into it.
*Munch, munch, munch*
Mmmmm, s' good!
I also made those pickles that you like so\n
much. They should be perfect about now.
Wow! Thank you! *Munch, munch, munch*
I recalled you liking these meatballs, too.\n
I do hope they are to your liking.
Homf--*munch, munch*...\n
Mmmmm, delicious!
Wait... did you make these, Sister?
I can cook well enough to make these, at least.
Never thought I'd see Kuon treated like a child.\n
But I guess to those two... she still is a\n
child.
It's a little embarrassing to watch them\n
pamper her like that.
C'mon, Haku, you should try some too.
Oh, yeah. I think I will, then...
Guess food from her homeland has a special\n
significance to her. She's in such a good mood\n
now...
You can put other food on top of the mororo\n
and eat it, or you can pour some of the sauce\n
on too!
The classy little party we were having quickly\n
turned into a sight I see at dinner every night.
Oh well. I guess this is fun all the same.
...This is even kind of nostalgic for me.
If I remember correctly, the first night after\n
I met Kuon... was kind of like this.
I've come a long way since then... but this\n
feeling is still the same.
Hmhm, I see your appetite has not changed.\n
It brings a smile to my face, seeing you eat\n
like you do.
I agree with you there. It makes all that\n
effort to prepare the food feel worthwhile.
Something about their calm gazes on Kuon...\n
It's like their eyes alone are brimming with\n
kindness.
No need to hold back now. You may eat all you\n
want.
Yes, we have plenty more where that came from.
Mnf--*munch, munch, munch, munch*...
Kuon continues to eat as she is told, her\n
expression childlike and full of bliss.
And beside her, the two women watch...
With eyes like those of proud mothers, fondly\n
looking upon their dear child.
